original_hypothesis.title: Missing Constructor Validation Enables Permanent Protocol Bricking via Zero or Malicious GaugeController Address
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The constructor accepts _gaugeController and _governance addresses without any validation (zero-address check, code-existence check, or interface support verification). If deployed with _gaugeController set to address(0) or a non-contract address, every call to update_market will revert on the external call to gauge_relative_weight_write, permanently bricking all claim and sync_ledger operations. Since gaugeController has no setter function, this misconfiguration is irrecoverable without full redeployment.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Unlike governance which has setGovernance for recovery, gaugeController is permanently fixed at construction. The constructor at lines 44-47 performs no validation on either address. While this requires a deployment error, the irreversibility and total protocol impact warrant flagging as a code defect (missing defensive validation on a critical, immutable parameter).
deployment_assumptions: Deployed with incorrect _gaugeController address (zero address or non-contract)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Constructor at LendingLedger.sol:44-47 performs no address validation; no deployment script provided to verify deployment parameters

proof_of_signal.pattern: constructor(address _gaugeController, address _governance) sets gaugeController = GaugeController(_gaugeController) with no require(_gaugeController != address(0)) or code-size check; no setter exists for recovery

preconditions: LendingLedger deployed with _gaugeController = address(0) or non-contract address
No ability to change gaugeController post-deployment

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: gaugeController.gauge_relative_weight_write(_market, epoch) called on potentially invalid address
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Deployment error — not an active attack but an irrecoverable misconfiguration
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit — all CANTO sent to the contract becomes permanently locked

economic_questions: Is there a deployment script that validates the GaugeController address?
What is the total value at risk if the protocol is deployed incorrectly?
Could a governance attacker use setGovernance to take control and then benefit from a bricked protocol?
validation_requirements: Review deployment scripts for address validation
Test constructor with address(0) to confirm permanent DoS

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: No profit — all contract funds become inaccessible

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All future users who deposit or send CANTO to the contract
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% loss of rewards and inability to sync positions
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All CANTO sent to the contract + all lending market integrations broken

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm revert behavior with invalid gaugeController

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
